incorporations.reason: Italian 'giaciglio di erbe' means a rough pallet (giaciglio) of grass/herbs (erbe), not straw. Gemini's 'pallet of grass' is more faithful to both words.
Italian 'contesta di fronde' means woven/interwoven (contesta) with fronds (fronde). 'Hung with branches' loses the sense of weaving and mistranslates fronde as branches.
Italian 'gingillo' specifically means a trinket or bauble — a small precious ornament. 'Objects' is too vague and loses the affectionate diminutive quality of the original.
